OLLAMA_PORT.description: Host port Ollama binds on. Default-bound to 127.0.0.1 (see template.yml — OLLAMA_HOST). For LAN/public access put NPM + Authelia in front, do not change this to 0.0.0.0.

OLLAMA_DEFAULT_MODEL.description: Primary model — the tag Hermes' `model.model` points at after install. Use any Ollama library tag (https://ollama.com/library) or a user-namespaced tag like `VladimirGav/gemma4-26b-16GB-VRAM:latest`. Default `gemma4:e4b` is the household-stack sweet spot: 8B params at Q4_K_M, ~10 GB on disk, fits 100% on a 16 GB GPU, AND ships native multimodal capabilities (`ollama show gemma4:e4b` reports `completion, vision, audio, tools, thinking`). That single tag covers what we used to need a separate vision pull for (#923 / #938).

OLLAMA_EXTRA_MODELS.description: Additional models to pre-pull on first install (comma-separated tags), on top of OLLAMA_DEFAULT_MODEL. The point is to give the operator one-click switchable alternatives in Hermes' Models tab without a fresh download. Default ships `VladimirGav/gemma4-26b-16GB-VRAM:latest` — a quantized 26B that still fits 100% on a 16 GB GPU, complementing the smaller default for harder text reasoning. CAVEAT: the aggressive quantization strips vision and audio from its `Capabilities` block (it reports `completion, tools, thinking` only). Switching the active model to this tag gives up multimodal — keep `gemma4:e4b` for OCR / voice-note flows, or pull plain `gemma4:26b` (~17 GB, partial CPU offload) which retains vision. Set to empty string to skip extras.

OLLAMA_VISION_MODEL.description: Optional second model pulled on first start, mainly historical now that the default OLLAMA_DEFAULT_MODEL (`gemma4:e4b`) ships vision + audio natively. Leave blank in normal household installs — only set this if you've changed OLLAMA_DEFAULT_MODEL to a text-only tag and still want OSCAR's `media-ingestion-multimodal` skill to have a vision backend. Suggested non-default tags: `qwen2.5vl:7b` (Apache-2, ~6 GB), `llava:13b` (~8 GB), `bakllava:7b` (~5 GB).

OLLAMA_GPU_PASSTHROUGH.description: Blank = auto-detect: post-deploy engages NVIDIA GPU passthrough when the host has a CDI-registered GPU (`/etc/cdi/nvidia.yaml`, from `nvidia-ctk cdi generate`), otherwise CPU-only. Set `yes` to force GPU or `no` to force CPU. Actual passthrough is delivered by post-deploy's #1026 fixup, which swaps the .kube unit for a `.container` Quadlet with `AddDevice=nvidia.com/gpu=all` (podman silently drops the manifest's `resources.limits.nvidia.com/gpu`, so the fixup — not the rendered block — is what gets the device).

OLLAMA_READINESS_TIMEOUT_SECONDS.description: How long post-deploy waits for the first model pull to finish. Multi-GB models on a slow connection can take 10+ minutes; the default leaves slack. Tune down for tiny models on fast links if you want faster install feedback.

OLLAMA_SUBDOMAIN.description: Subdomain for the Ollama API. Internal exposure — gets a cert and a LAN-only access list, plus Authelia forward-auth on top because Ollama itself ships no auth. Use https://<OLLAMA_SUBDOMAIN>.<PUBLIC_DOMAIN>/api/generate from any LAN client; mobile/external clients get a 403 from NPM and a 302-to-Authelia from any in-LAN unauthenticated request.
